name: Pineapple/Nut Passover Sponge Cake --
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 tsp Minced lemon and orange zest
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F.
2. Line the bottom of a 9-inch tube pan with a doughnut-shaped piece of wax paper, notching around the center circle every inch. Do not grease the pan.
3. Sift the first four ingredients into a bowl and set aside.
4. Separate eggs: add 4 yolks to one mixing machine bowl and 10 whites to another.
5. Using the whisk attachment, beat egg whites on medium-high speed until foamy. Turn the speed up to high and sprinkle in half the sugar, beating until stiff and glossy.
6. Set the bowl with yolks aside.
7. In a separate bowl, beat the remaining eggs until light. Sprinkle in the remaining sugar, orange zest, and pineapple concentrate. Beat on high speed until well blended.
8. Using a wooden spoon, stir about half a cup of beaten whites into the yolk mixture. Then pour all of the yolk mixture over the beaten egg whites and fold by hand using 12 sweeping strokes.
9. Sift the dry ingredients again over the batter and continue folding for another 12 strokes. Fold in the nuts (another 12 strokes would suffice).
10. Spoon the batter into the prepared pan and smooth the top. Run a blunt knife around the sides to release any air bubbles.
11. Bake in the center of the oven for 55 minutes, or until the top is lightly browned and a toothpick inserted comes out clean.
12. Remove from the oven and invert the tube onto a 3-cup mason jar with a 1 ½-inch neck or any convenient holder that will fit the tube. Cool for 1 ½ hours.
13. Gently unmold. Peel off the wax paper. When completely cooled, dust with confectioner's sugar. Cut with a sharp serrated knife or pull apart servings with a fork.
